issues.description: compute_scope runs ~8-10 SQLite queries per call (6 branches: company, visible_department_ids, managed_department_ids, visible_task_ids, own_employee_id, private_visible_employee_ids). Called on every permission check (tool execution, knowledge query, workspace access). §4.5 sets <10ms release_gate but explicitly forbids cross-request caching. With 100 employees and deep department trees, cold-cache queries may exceed 10ms. The design does not specify warm-cache vs cold-cache measurement conditions.

Lease-acquire is a single SQLite transaction scanning all backends' held counts + global total + queue heads + lease creation + queue update. §4.5 targets <50ms release_gate. Under concurrent load (many tasks starting/completing simultaneously), SQLite single-writer serialization creates contention. With global_process_limit up to 64 and multiple companies, the GROUP BY on backend_leases WHERE status=held could be slow.

Knowledge search has 7 sequential steps: compute_scope → FTS5 → LanceDB (parallel) → RRF fusion → governance priority → token trim → audit write. For interactive chat, this runs on every turn with knowledge retrieval. §4.5 has no observational metric for knowledge_search_e2e_time, so no baseline will be measured at release.

Backup snapshot barrier freezes all writes while waiting for in-flight transactions, active turns to checkpoint, and outbox to quiesce. Quiesce timeout value not specified. During freeze, all RPC write operations retry with SYS-BACKUP-IN-PROGRESS. Long-running tool executions (30+ seconds) could cause substantial barrier wait.

session_threads lookup by (employee_id, security_context_key) is the primary access path for session.sendMessage without thread_id. The ER diagram and §15 do not explicitly declare a composite index or UNIQUE constraint on this pair. Without it, lookup degrades to full table scan per employee.

Outbox Worker polling interval not specified. 30+ named events depend on timely delivery: knowledge extraction on TaskCompleted, capability metrics on TaskCompleted/ReviewCompleted, UI notifications. Too-slow polling adds artificial latency; too-fast polling adds SQLite read pressure.

Every model call dispatch requires: read provider_model_prices, compute worst-case cost with int64 overflow check, write usage_reservations, SUM(amount_micros) for task+currency to check budget. The SUM aggregation runs on every dispatch. For tasks with many parallel workers, this becomes a contention point.

transcript.jsonl is append-only with no total size limit or archival strategy. context-summary.md checkpoints compress older messages but raw transcript grows unboundedly. For long-running sessions (months of history), recovery requires reading the entire file to find the latest valid checkpoint, becoming increasingly expensive.

Every API Provider call requires a cross-process UDS round-trip for credential resolution: Sidecar signs capability → Rust verifies → looks up Keychain → returns plaintext. Single-use 30s TTL means no connection pooling. High-throughput parallel workers using API Providers multiply this overhead.

Five separate audit tables (acl_audit_log, knowledge_access_logs, org_change_audit, knowledge_governance_audit, backend_change_audit) are written on different operations. Each adds schema complexity and index maintenance. Acceptable for v1 baseline but note for future scale.

sidecar_restart_ready_time <3s release_gate does not specify time budget breakdown (process spawn, Python init, migration check, RPC bind). P0-T3 DoD references this metric but does not break down the budget. Cold PyInstaller startup could add 1-2s; test should run against frozen binary, not uv run.

PV-13 checks Backend health/lease state for each agent/review/fix/merge node. With max_nodes=200, this adds per-node DB queries. Should batch Backend health lookups rather than querying per-node. Not a blocker given scale limits, but worth noting.

If chosen LanceDB version only supports post-filter, the system falls back to computing similarity against all vectors before filtering. At 10K document scale, this wastes significant compute and returns potentially incorrect results. §17 lists pre-filter validation as quality gate but timing (Phase 0 vs later) should be explicit.

Default global_process_limit = clamp(logical_cpu_count, 2, 16). Each CLI Provider sub-process (e.g., Claude Code CLI with Node.js) consumes significant memory. With 16GB RAM and 12 sub-processes, memory pressure could cause swapping. CPU-based default may need memory-based adjustment for Apple Silicon.
